translation.en: I will give you the document.

content.sentence: Tôi sẽ ___ tài liệu cho bạn.

notes:
đưa usually means to give or to hand something to someone. It often implies moving something from one person/place to another.

It’s a very flexible verb: you can đưa + object + cho + person when giving something physically. It’s also used for taking someone somewhere (like “bring/drive/escort”) and for abstract things like đưa ra ý kiến (give/put forward an opinion).

Close cousin: cho = “give” too, but đưa feels more like physically passing or transferring something.
exampleSentences: Anh có thể đưa cho em cuốn sách trên bàn không?
Chị nhớ đưa chìa khóa cho mẹ trước khi đi làm nhé.
Em sẽ đưa giấy này cho giáo viên vào ngày mai.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you give me the book on the table?
Remember to give the key to mom before going to work.
I will give this paper to the teacher tomorrow.

translation.en: I will give you the document.

content.sentence: Tôi sẽ ___ tài liệu cho bạn.

definitions.en: to take, to bring, to deliver

example.en: I will take you to school.
example.vi: Tôi sẽ đưa bạn đến trường.

notes_md:
**đưa** usually means **to give** or **to hand something to someone**. It often implies moving something from one person/place to another.

It’s a very flexible verb: you can **đưa + object + cho + person** when giving something physically. It’s also used for **taking someone somewhere** (like “bring/drive/escort”) and for abstract things like **đưa ra ý kiến** (give/put forward an opinion).


Close cousin: **cho** = “give” too, but **đưa** feels more like physically passing or transferring something.
